message:
The other day, I found myself rummaging through old boxes in the attic and stumbled upon a stack of board games. Remember those? It was like rediscovering treasure! 🤗 I invited the family for a game night, and we ended up laughing and enjoying each other’s company without screens for hours. It reminded me how important it is to have some low-tech fun, especially in times where we might feel a little disconnected.

As we played, I couldn’t help but think about how important it is to be prepared as a family, not just in terms of plans and supplies, but also in creating those moments together. So, while we’ve got our go-bags ready, let’s not forget to fill our lives with laughter too. 

Have you dusted off any old games or hobbies lately? What’s your favorite way to unplug and connect?
